prompt: Professional infographic titled "5 Stages of Product Development". Style: bold editorial with vibrant color blocking and high-contrast typography. Layout: top section features the title in large bold white text on a deep charcoal background (#1A1A2E) with a bold subtitle 'From Idea to Launch'; below the title, five vertically stacked stage cards fill the remaining canvas, each card occupying equal height, separated by thin white divider lines; each card has a large bold stage number on the left (01 through 05), the stage name in extra-bold uppercase type in the center, and a single-line descriptor below the name in smaller regular weight text; footer at bottom shows 'Product Development Framework' in small white caption text centered. Color palette: each stage card uses a distinct bold background color — Stage 1 Discovery: electric violet #7B2FBE; Stage 2 Design: vivid coral #FF4D6D; Stage 3 Development: bright amber #FFBE0B; Stage 4 Testing: electric teal #06D6A0; Stage 5 Launch: electric blue #118AB2; all text within colored cards is white #FFFFFF; title section background is deep charcoal #1A1A2E. Typography: stage numbers rendered at 80pt extra-bold sans-serif; stage names rendered at 52pt bold uppercase sans-serif; stage descriptors rendered at 20pt regular sans-serif; title rendered at 56pt bold white sans-serif; all text must be perfectly legible at full resolution with zero anti-aliasing blur. Data content: Stage 01 — DISCOVERY: Understand the problem and user needs; Stage 02 — DESIGN: Craft wireframes, prototypes, and UX flows; Stage 03 — DEVELOPMENT: Build features and write production code; Stage 04 — TESTING: QA, bug fixes, and performance validation; Stage 05 — LAUNCH: Ship to users and monitor adoption. Visual elements: large bold numerals (01-05) left-aligned in each card acting as visual anchors; thin 2px white horizontal dividers between each stage card; no icons or illustrations — typography and color blocking carry all visual weight. Quality directive: ultra-sharp, print-quality detail, pixel-perfect element alignment, zero text rendering errors. Do not add decorative elements not specified. Render all text as clean, unblurred, perfectly legible typography. Negative: blurry text, garbled typography, illegible fonts, inconsistent color palette, random decorative clutter, misaligned elements, overlapping labels, stock photo aesthetic, photorealistic faces, lens flare, bokeh, photography lighting, beauty filters, skin textures.
